name: Meta Progression
description: Progress that persists beyond a single run, match, character life, or session and changes future attempts.
design_notes: Meta progression changes the meaning of failure and repetition. It needs a clean boundary between run_progression_state and account_progression_state, plus migration-safe unlocks, currency sources, and caps so repeated attempts feel rewarding without invalidating mastery.
design_purpose: Give repeated runs or sessions durable meaning.
Unlock options, modifiers, cosmetics, or power that shape future play.
Provide a long-term structure for roguelikes, live games, idle games, and account-based progression.
player_fantasy: The player feels that even failed attempts leave knowledge, resources, or account growth behind.

parameters.name: account_progression_state_id
parameters.description: Persistence scope for unlocks and currencies that survive individual runs.

parameters.name: run_progression_state_id
parameters.description: Temporary state reset when the run, match, or character attempt ends.

parameters.name: permanent_unlock_flag
parameters.description: Whether a reward persists across resets or only affects the active run.

parameters.name: meta_currency_source_id
parameters.description: Source identifier for currency or points added to account progression.

parameters.name: catchup_multiplier_cap
parameters.description: Limit for boosts used to help returning or behind players progress faster.

edge_cases: A run ends during a crash or disconnect and must decide which rewards leave run_progression_state.
A permanent unlock is removed or rebalanced in a patch and needs compensation or migration.
Players share a device but need separate account_progression_state records.
Meta upgrades can make early content too easy for new players if applied to tutorials or onboarding.
common_bugs: Run-only buffs leak into account_progression_state and become permanent.
Meta currency is granted on both run end and result screen reopen because reward transaction IDs are not stored.
Save migration resets permanent_unlock_flag values when unlock definitions move.
Client-side account progression is trusted in multiplayer and allows edited unlock state.
balancing_notes: Meta progression should reduce frustration without making player skill irrelevant.
Permanent power needs caps, branching choices, or escalating costs to avoid flattening the difficulty curve.
Cosmetic and option unlocks can provide long-term goals without increasing balance pressure.
accessibility_notes: Make the difference between temporary run rewards and permanent account rewards explicit in UI text.
Offer progress summaries after a run so players can review gains at their own pace.

implementation_notes.unity: Separate ScriptableObject definitions from saved account_progression_state; commit run-end rewards with transaction_id records before clearing run_progression_state.
implementation_notes.godot: Use separate Resources or dictionaries for run and account state; migration scripts should preserve permanent_unlock_flag values by stable unlock IDs.
implementation_notes.unreal: Store account-level unlocks in SaveGame or backend profile data, while run state lives in GameState or PlayerState; server authority is required for shared progression rewards.
implementation_notes.web: Use distinct persisted stores for account and run state, and make run-end reward commits idempotent so refreshes cannot grant meta currency twice.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Uses persistent resources and unlocks between runs.

example_games.title: Dead Cells
example_games.note: Uses unlocks and upgrades that persist across runs.
